assets.prompt:
Lucien saved another woman first. His Luna was bleeding in the snow.

Evelyn Ashford had spent years holding Blackthorn Pack together. But when the mountain collapsed, her right hand was crushed under stone while she protected medical supplies.

Lucien arrived by helicopter, saw her trapped, and still ordered, "Take Seraphina first." Then he said Evelyn could reach the shelter line herself. She crawled back alive and found Seraphina wrapped in the black-and-gold Luna blanket, leaning against him like the wife he had chosen.

Feverish and bandaged, Evelyn returned to the Manor and reached for Caspian. Her own son hid behind Seraphina. He said she smelled like medicine and blood, then gave Seraphina the charm meant for his mother.

Still, she gave Lucien one last excuse. Maybe he had not understood her injury. Outside his study, the doctor warned that her hand might need microsurgery. Lucien only asked if she could still stay in the Manor. Then came the verdict: "It is not that important."

That was when she stopped begging the house to love her. With her right hand useless, Evelyn used her left to write the severance request. She removed the Luna ring, left the master key, and packed only what was hers.

Lucien did not even read it. He asked where she was staying, then told the butler, "Burn it." The letter curled black in the fireplace while he declared, "Blackthorn Pack has no discarded Luna."

Evelyn did not cry. She understood peaceful leaving would never work. So the next time she leaves, it will be in a way he cannot burn.
